metrics.narrative: The business problem is well articulated (Coupa's Invoice Smash module handling only 5-10% of invoices, forcing manual AP processing, and a customer-service decline post-Thoma Bravo acquisition), but no quantified savings or ROI figure has been stated. Elie Sauma was explicit on 15 May 2026: 'I'm not looking to save money. I'm just looking to replace Coupa, and if we're within the budget we are today, I'm okay with this... the key is to have a functioning tool that's better than Coupa.' The value case is qualitative parity-plus-fit, not a quantified metric; building the TCO/ROI model for Elie's mid-July business case is the open move.

champion.narrative: No internal champion has surfaced. Elie Sauma is the Decision Maker but functions as a neutral evaluator, not an advocate: he gave corrective (not enthusiastic) demo feedback on 15 May 2026 ('I think you guys try to show everything, and I would have preferred that you guys just focused on the stuff we wanted') and has offered no stated preference over the three other blind-RFP finalists. Harsh Sejpal and Anson Malanakarot are engaged on logistics and technical questions but have shown no advocacy behavior (introductions, pushing back on rivals, driving the agenda) on any call to date. Developing a genuine internal advocate before the 15 Jul 2026 downselect is an open, unaddressed need.

competition.narrative: Coupa is the incumbent being actively displaced after 5-6 years, driven by the relevant stakeholder failure and post-Thoma Bravo service decline (Elie Sauma, 9 Apr 2026). Zycus was named an automatic finalist via a trusted referral rather than open competition, and was reconfirmed among the top 4 finalists on 15 May 2026. However, the RFP is genuinely blind: three other finalists remain entirely unnamed, and Zycus has zero visibility into their positioning, pricing, or advocacy. The wedge is the unified Merlin platform vs. Coupa's bolted-on acquisitions, but this must still be proven against unknown rivals at the 15 Jul 2026 downselect.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well documented and buyer-articulated: 'Invoice Smash has just not worked for us as a company... Coupa themselves admitted it's not working... yet they would not give us credit back' (Elie Sauma, 9 Apr 2026), 'only 5% or 10% of our invoices are going through Invoice Smash' with the rest processed manually via AP mailboxes (Elie Sauma, 9 Apr 2026), and a stated decline in Coupa's service and innovation post-Thoma Bravo acquisition. A secondary, confirmed gap is ESG/supplier-risk monitoring, currently plugged by the third-party tool Relich rather than natively in Coupa. The pain owner is Elie Sauma and his procurement team, with the AP automation pain also owned by Sitecore's (unnamed) senior VP of accounting.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics (MSA, order form, procurement channel, InfoSec) have been discussed; Elie explicitly deferred all commercial/contract conversation until after the 15 Jul 2026 downselect ('we can have those in negotiations. I'm not too concerned'). Visibility here will only emerge once Zycus clears the downselect and enters negotiation; until then this is a genuine unknown, not a hygiene gap.

economic_buyer.narrative: Elie Sauma (VP, Head of Procurement & Travel) clearly controls the vendor selection and pricing-parity conversation, telling Zycus on 15 May 2026 'do you mind sharing a range... we will help create a business case to further justify the ROI' and confirming he alone will present the business case internally. However, the true final budget approver is Sitecore's CFO, who has never appeared on a call, is not mapped as a Salesforce contact, and per Elie's 15 May 2026 statement will be presented to 'in mid-July' with no Zycus presence. The economic buyer role is split: Elie controls the selection recommendation, but the CFO holds the final sign-off and is unengaged.

decision_process.narrative: The call sequence itself maps the process: intro call (9 Apr 2026) to establish fit, first demo (30 Apr 2026), a check-in call confirming down-select to 4 finalists (15 May 2026), formal RFP release (9 Jun 2026, responses due 24 Jun), Zycus's submitted response (29-30 Jun 2026), and a downselect deadline of 15 Jul 2026. Post-downselect, Elie plans a financial/AP-focused demo, then negotiation, then his own internal business-case presentation to the CFO in mid-July, targeting a decision by end of July and transition Aug 2026-Mar 2027. This is a buyer-run, RFP-gated process with Elie as the sole visible approval gate before the CFO.

decision_criteria.narrative: Elie named the evaluation focus explicitly on 15 May 2026: future demos should center on 'the financial teams... especially accounts payable' plus 'vendor onboarding and OFAC capabilities,' and he confirmed RFP instructions would formalize this ('I'll have some instruction... to guide the focus of the demo'). This was corrective feedback on the 30 Apr 2026 first demo, which he felt was too broad ('I think you guys try to show everything, and I would have preferred that you guys focused on the stuff we wanted'). The criteria are confirmed and narrowly scoped, not inferred.

items.detail: Elie's budget constraint is 'comparable to current spend with Coupa' and 'stay within the budget of what we have today' (15 May). He is not cost-minimizing ('not looking for the cheaper solution... I'm gonna go with the best tool possible within my budget'), but he will not approve a price above the current Coupa spend plus migration cost. If another finalist prices significantly lower OR if Zycus prices above Coupa equivalence, the deal is at risk. No pricing discussion has occurred yet; commercials are post-down-select (after 15 Jul). Migration cost (transition services, training, data migration) must be factored into the total TCO and presented as part of the business case, not as a surprise add-on.

Elie emphasized the importance of migration ease ('beside the tool and the AI component, there's also obviously the migration away from Coupa... the ease of doing that... we're really gonna need to kind of convince people, especially leadership', 9 Apr) and training ('gives us from Aug till March next year to work on transition and all that... training and all that stuff', 15 May). Sitecore is mature on Coupa (5-6 years, multi-module, deep integrations with Microsoft/Azure), so a migration mid-contract is high-risk. If Zycus cannot demonstrate a smooth, low-disruption migration plan with comprehensive training and change management support, leadership could veto the switch and stay with Coupa despite dissatisfaction. This is a make-or-break criterion, especially given PE cost pressure ('things are super tight', Elie, 9 Apr).

The CFO (who approves the business case and final budget per Elie's timeline, 15 May) has never appeared on a call, is not mapped as a Salesforce contact, and has no relationship with the relevant stakeholder will present the business case to the CFO in mid-July alone, with no Zycus presence or prior CFO engagement. If the CFO is risk-averse, has a relationship with another finalist, or is not convinced by Elie's presentation (lacking a quantified ROI case Zycus has not yet delivered), the CFO could veto the migration. No exec sponsor above Elie is visible. Risk: the deal could die in the CFO room with no Zycus ability to influence.

Zycus is one of 4 finalists in a blind RFP with no competitive intel on the other 3. One of those finalists may have a champion, a stronger RFP response, a lower price, or executive relationships Zycus lacks. Elie is a neutral evaluator, not an advocate — he has given no signal that Zycus is preferred, and he gave corrective demo feedback ('you guys tried to show everything', 15 May) suggesting Zycus did not fully meet expectations. If another finalist has a coach who is selling Zycus's weaknesses when we are not in the room, or if that finalist better aligned their RFP response to the evaluation criteria (AP focus, vendor onboarding/OFAC, AI proof), Zycus could lose on 15 Jul. We have no visibility into the competitive dynamics.

The down-select is 15 Jul 2026 (9 days from today). If Zycus does not advance, the deal is lost. Post-down-select, follow-on demos (for financial/AP teams), negotiations, and contract award are all TBD with no committed dates. Elie wants a decision by end-July and transition/training Aug 2026 - Mar 2027, but if the down-select slips, the demos slip, or negotiations extend (common in PE-backed orgs with tight budgets), the close date (12 Aug) will slip, potentially into Q4 2026 or beyond. The timeline is buyer-controlled and aggressive; any delay in down-select or demo scheduling puts the Aug close at risk.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Sitecore's stated expectations align well with Zycus's AI/Microsoft-native positioning, with one clear exposure: demo scope discipline.

hard.ais_why:
• Elie Sauma described AI-first leadership on the 4/9/2026 call: leadership has embraced AI-driven automation across procurement, and his team has already built in-house automation using Microsoft Forms, Lists, Power Automate, and the Coupa API at zero spend. They've deployed two Copilot agents covering procurement and travel inquiries, and they plan to import Zycus training materials into that Copilot environment.
• Sitecore asked Zycus to dig into the AP side, specifically AI invoice scanning, and noted that their SVP has committed to automating AP invoice processing to replace manual email scanning into Coupa. After Hemant walked through AP Smartdesk, Elie's team flagged interest in AI-enabled invoice extraction to auto-populate eInvoice fields, again buyer-pulled.
• They also stated a requirement for AI-driven ESG risk monitoring across anti-slavery, anti-bribery, and supplier compliance, and they've built an intake system inside Microsoft Teams, which maps directly to Merlin Intake on Teams. With 90%+ Azure infrastructure, a formal RFP planned, and demo scheduling moving inside a week, this is a textbook AI Hungry profile.

deltas.to: Elie's budget constraint is 'comparable to current spend with Coupa' and 'stay within the budget of what we have today' (15 May). He is not cost-minimizing ('no
deltas.subject: Elie's budget constraint is 'comparable to current spend with Coupa' and 'stay within the budget of what we have today' (15 May). He is not cost-minimizing ('not looking for the cheaper solution... I'm gonna go with the best tool possible within my budget'), but he will not approve a price above the current Coupa spend plus migration cost. If another finalist prices significantly lower OR if Zycus prices above Coupa equivalence, the deal is at risk. No pricing discussion has occurred yet; commercials are post-down-select (after 15 Jul). Migration cost (transition services, training, data migration) must be factored into the total TCO and presented as part of the business case, not as a surprise add-on.

deltas.to: Elie emphasized the importance of migration ease ('beside the tool and the AI component, there's also obviously the migration away from Coupa... the ease of doi
deltas.subject: Elie emphasized the importance of migration ease ('beside the tool and the AI component, there's also obviously the migration away from Coupa... the ease of doing that... we're really gonna need to kind of convince people, especially leadership', 9 Apr) and training ('gives us from Aug till March next year to work on transition and all that... training and all that stuff', 15 May). Sitecore is mature on Coupa (5-6 years, multi-module, deep integrations with Microsoft/Azure), so a migration mid-contract is high-risk. If Zycus cannot demonstrate a smooth, low-disruption migration plan with comprehensive training and change management support, leadership could veto the switch and stay with Coupa despite dissatisfaction. This is a make-or-break criterion, especially given PE cost pressure ('things are super tight', Elie, 9 Apr).

deltas.to: The CFO (who approves the business case and final budget per Elie's timeline, 15 May) has never appeared on a call, is not mapped as a Salesforce contact, and h
deltas.subject: The CFO (who approves the business case and final budget per Elie's timeline, 15 May) has never appeared on a call, is not mapped as a Salesforce contact, and has no relationship with the relevant stakeholder will present the business case to the CFO in mid-July alone, with no Zycus presence or prior CFO engagement. If the CFO is risk-averse, has a relationship with another finalist, or is not convinced by Elie's presentation (lacking a quantified ROI case Zycus has not yet delivered), the CFO could veto the migration. No exec sponsor above Elie is visible. Risk: the deal could die in the CFO room with no Zycus ability to influence.

deltas.to: Zycus is one of 4 finalists in a blind RFP with no competitive intel on the other 3. One of those finalists may have a champion, a stronger RFP response, a lowe
deltas.subject: Zycus is one of 4 finalists in a blind RFP with no competitive intel on the other 3. One of those finalists may have a champion, a stronger RFP response, a lower price, or executive relationships Zycus lacks. Elie is a neutral evaluator, not an advocate — he has given no signal that Zycus is preferred, and he gave corrective demo feedback ('you guys tried to show everything', 15 May) suggesting Zycus did not fully meet expectations. If another finalist has a coach who is selling Zycus's weaknesses when we are not in the room, or if that finalist better aligned their RFP response to the evaluation criteria (AP focus, vendor onboarding/OFAC, AI proof), Zycus could lose on 15 Jul. We have no visibility into the competitive dynamics.

value.detail: Elie's budget constraint is 'comparable to current spend with Coupa' and 'stay within the budget of what we have today' (15 May). He is not cost-minimizing ('not looking for the cheaper solution... I'm gonna go with the best tool possible within my budget'), but he will not approve a price above the current Coupa spend plus migration cost. If another finalist prices significantly lower OR if Zycus prices above Coupa equivalence, the deal is at risk. No pricing discussion has occurred yet; commercials are post-down-select (after 15 Jul). Migration cost (transition services, training, data migration) must be factored into the total TCO and presented as part of the business case, not as a surprise add-on.

value.detail: Elie emphasized the importance of migration ease ('beside the tool and the AI component, there's also obviously the migration away from Coupa... the ease of doing that... we're really gonna need to kind of convince people, especially leadership', 9 Apr) and training ('gives us from Aug till March next year to work on transition and all that... training and all that stuff', 15 May). Sitecore is mature on Coupa (5-6 years, multi-module, deep integrations with Microsoft/Azure), so a migration mid-contract is high-risk. If Zycus cannot demonstrate a smooth, low-disruption migration plan with comprehensive training and change management support, leadership could veto the switch and stay with Coupa despite dissatisfaction. This is a make-or-break criterion, especially given PE cost pressure ('things are super tight', Elie, 9 Apr).
value.source: Avoma intro call 9 Apr, Avoma call 15 May, Elie Sauma

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is a confirmed automatic finalist in Sitecore's blind Source-to-Pay RFP displacing an underperforming Coupa deployment, but must clear a 15 Jul 2026 downselect against three unnamed competitors with no internal champion pushing for us and the CFO who approves the July business case never engaged.

deal_risk.text: The 15 Jul 2026 downselect, 8 days away, could eliminate Zycus with zero competitive intelligence on the three other blind-RFP finalists.
Sitecore's 5-6 year deep Coupa integration makes migration ease and change management a stated make-or-break factor for internal leadership buy-in that Zycus has not yet demonstrated.

win_position.text: Elie Sauma named Zycus an automatic finalist from a trusted referral and confirmed on 15 May 2026 that Zycus has 'everything we need' from the demo.

No internal champion is actively advocating for Zycus: Elie is a neutral evaluator who gave corrective demo feedback ('you guys tried to show everything', 15 May) and has given no preference signal over three other unnamed RFP finalists.
The CFO who must approve the mid-July business case has never engaged with Zycus and Elie plans to present alone, leaving Zycus with no influence in the final budget decision.

deal_momentum.text: Zycus met the RFP process on schedule, submitting vendor-question responses by the 30 Jun EOD deadline and receiving buyer confirmation by email on 1 Jul 2026.

No Zycus-Sitecore call has occurred since 15 May 2026 (about 7 weeks); all recent progress (RFP release 9 Jun, submission 29-30 Jun) is tracked only through logged next-step notes, not fresh buyer conversation.

customer_commitment.text: Elie confirmed on 15 May 2026 he is not seeking the cheapest option but 'the best tool possible within budget,' favoring fit over pure price competition.
Elie's budget bar is strict parity with current Coupa spend plus migration cost, and no pricing conversation has occurred; commercials are deferred until after the 15 Jul downselect.
